extractions.problem: Identification of small-molecule inhibitors targeting the urokinase receptor (uPAR) to block breast tumor metastasis.
extractions.method: Virtual screening of uPAR, followed by synthesis, biochemical assays, cell-based invasion/migration/adhesion assays, angiogenesis assays, pharmacokinetic characterization, and in vivo mouse metastasis model.
extractions.finding: Compound IPR-69 showed leadlike properties, oral bioavailability (peak ~40 μM, t1/2 ~2h), 20% tumor volume reduction, and reduced metastasis in NOD-SCID mice.
extractions.dataset: MDA-MB-231 breast cancer cells, NOD-SCID mice with TMD-231 mammary fat pad inoculation
extractions.metric: IC50 (invasion, migration, adhesion, angiogenesis, cell growth)
extractions.result: IC50 ~30 μM (invasion/migration), IC50 3 μM (angiogenesis), IC50 6-18 μM (growth), 20% tumor volume reduction in vivo

extractions.problem: Scheduling operations in virtual manufacturing cells with outsourcing and sequence-dependent setup times to minimize makespan and transportation cost.
extractions.method: Bi-objective mixed integer programming solved by ε-constraint method (exact) and a bi-objective genetic algorithm (GA) for large-scale NP-hard instances.
extractions.finding: The proposed model outperforms base models, and the GA obtains efficient solutions in much less computational time compared to the ε-constraint method.
extractions.dataset: Numerical examples of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling problems
extractions.metric: makespan, total transportation cost, computational time
extractions.baseline: ε-constraint exact method, base scheduling models
extractions.result: GA achieves comparable solution quality in much less computational time

extractions.problem: Reducing energy consumption in ultradense small cell networks by maximizing the number of small cells in sleeping state via wireless virtualization.
extractions.method: Wireless resource mapping algorithm based on virtual cell division and alternate updating of small cell mapping and resource allocation.
extractions.finding: The algorithm effectively reduces system energy consumption and required wireless resource amount while satisfying users' QoS constraints.
extractions.dataset: Simulated ultradense small cell network
extractions.metric: energy consumption, wireless resource utilization, convergence
extractions.result: Effective reduction in system energy consumption while maintaining QoS

extractions.problem: Minimizing overall transmit power in a virtual-cell downlink with distributed antennas while satisfying QoS constraints.
extractions.method: Sequential power allocation for single-user case; successive convex approximation (single condensation method) for multi-user case, accounting for both channel estimation and data transmission power.
extractions.finding: The proposed power allocation strategies achieve up to 45% power savings compared to equal power allocation baseline.
extractions.dataset: Simulated distributed wireless communication system
extractions.metric: total transmit power savings
extractions.result: up to 45% power savings

extractions.problem: Understanding how elastic coupling between cells in the inner ear enhances hearing across vertebrate species.
extractions.method: Yoking real and virtual cells to test cochlear amplification theory.
extractions.finding: Elastic coupling between cells in the inner ear enhances the hearing of amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals, confirming cochlear amplification theory.
extractions.dataset: Inner ear cells of amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals

extractions.problem: Describing how molecular components interact to create complex cellular behavior underlying development and disease, after cataloguing individual components.
extractions.method: Review of bioinformatics and genomics approaches for modeling the virtual cell, including systems-level interaction mapping.
extractions.finding: The next challenge after component cataloguing is describing component interactions to understand complex cellular behavior in development and disease.

extractions.problem: Batch splitting in scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells with capacity-constrained bottleneck machines.
extractions.method: Bi-level multi-objective mathematical model with separate decision levels for bottleneck and non-bottleneck machines.
extractions.finding: The bi-level model provides scheduling results and batch splitting strategies that maximize bottleneck machine utilization while improving additional objectives at the second level.
extractions.dataset: Application example of virtual manufacturing cell scheduling
extractions.metric: bottleneck machine utilization, scheduling objectives

extractions.problem: Providing deep proteomic profiling of mpkCCD collecting duct cells as a data resource and characterizing aberrant protein mobility on SDS-PAGE.
extractions.method: Three levels of fractionation (differential centrifugation, SDS-PAGE, HPLC) followed by tandem mass spectrometry; virtual Western blots generated from gel slice mapping.
extractions.finding: Identified 6,766 proteins over eight orders of magnitude abundance; >15% run aberrantly on SDS-PAGE due to various mechanisms; discovered a previously unrecognized aquaporin-2 isoform (<20 kDa).
extractions.dataset: mpkCCD mouse collecting duct cell line, 34.6 GB spectral data
extractions.metric: number of proteins identified, apparent vs calculated molecular weight agreement
extractions.result: 6,766 proteins identified, >15% aberrant mobility

extractions.problem: Minimizing end-to-end power consumption of cell-free massive MIMO deployed on virtualized cloud radio access networks (V-CRAN).
extractions.method: Joint optimization of radio, optical fronthaul, and virtualized cloud processing resources via mixed binary second-order cone programming solved by branch-and-bound.
extractions.finding: Cell-free massive MIMO substantially increases maximum rate with almost the same energy per bit, and is more power-efficient than conventional small-cell V-CRAN above 1 bit/s/Hz spectral efficiency.
extractions.dataset: Simulated V-CRAN with cell-free massive MIMO
extractions.metric: end-to-end power consumption, spectral efficiency, energy per bit
extractions.result: more power-efficient above 1 bit/s/Hz spectral efficiency

extractions.problem: Security analysis of mobile edge computing in virtualized small cell networks.
Discovering potent and selective CYP1A1 enzyme inhibitors for cancer chemoprevention via virtual screening.
extractions.method: Combined ligand-based e-pharmacophore screening and structure-based virtual screening (docking, MMGB/SA binding affinity) of 50,000 compounds, validated in vitro in Sacchrosomes and live human cells, with molecular dynamics simulations.
extractions.finding: Compound 5121780 potently and selectively inhibits CYP1 enzymes (IC50 269/30/56 nM for CYP1A1/1A2/1B1) with no detectable CYP2/CYP3 inhibition at 10 μM, rescued cells from B[a]P toxicity and overcame cisplatin resistance.
extractions.dataset: 50,000 compound library, CYP1A1-Sacchrosomes, CYP-expressing HEK293 cells
extractions.metric: % inhibition at 10 μM, IC50
extractions.result: IC50 = 269 nM (CYP1A1), 30 nM (CYP1A2), 56 nM (CYP1B1); 97% inhibition at 10 μM; 32/300 hits >50% inhibition

extractions.problem: Comparing software packages for kinetic modeling of biochemical networks to guide tool selection.
extractions.method: Comparison of 12 software packages on functionality, reliability, efficiency, user-friendliness, compatibility, SBML support, and multicompartmentalization.
extractions.finding: GEPASI is best for non-expert users with simple models; Jarnac/JDesigner preferred for large-scale models; Virtual Cell is the most versatile with clearest multicompartmentalization support. SBML compatibility is problematic in many packages.
extractions.dataset: 12 kinetic modeling software packages
extractions.metric: functionality, reliability, efficiency, user-friendliness, SBML compatibility
extractions.result: Virtual Cell most versatile; GEPASI best for non-experts

extractions.problem: Scheduling of virtual manufacturing cells using mixed integer programming.
Identifying novel nonpeptidic inhibitors of human thymidylate synthase (hTS) for ovarian cancer treatment.
extractions.method: Virtual screening targeting hTS, followed by enzymatic inhibition assays, X-ray crystallography, and cancer cell line activity testing.
extractions.finding: Compound 5 is the first nonpeptidic inhibitor binding the inactive hTS conformation, with Ki of 1.3 μM and anticancer activity against ovarian cancer cells matching cisplatin potency.
extractions.dataset: Human thymidylate synthase, ovarian cancer cell lines
extractions.metric: Ki, anticancer potency comparison
extractions.result: Ki = 1.3 μM, same potency as cisplatin against ovarian cancer cells

extractions.problem: Obtaining better representation of human proteome information by combining virtual and experimental 2DE approaches.
extractions.method: Experimental 2DE coupled with ESI LC-MS/MS and MALDI-TOF MS protein identification; comparison of experimental vs theoretical pI/Mw parameters.
extractions.finding: Major proteoforms in HepG2 cells have pI/Mw close to theoretical values, while minor protein species are heavily modified; modifications are even more pronounced in plasma proteome.
extractions.dataset: HEPG2 cell line, human blood plasma
extractions.metric: correlation between theoretical and experimental pI/Mw
extractions.baseline: theoretical pI/Mw from Compute pI/Mw tool
extractions.result: major proteoforms match theoretical; minor species heavily modified

extractions.problem: Identifying key challenges for broadcasting virtual reality content to large audiences using 5G millimeter wave small cell networks.
extractions.method: Performance evaluation using realistic 5G network settings to assess technology potential and practical constraints for VR broadcast.
extractions.finding: VR broadcast can be supported under ideal conditions using 5G mmWave small cells with slightly higher system bandwidth requirements.
extractions.dataset: Simulated 5G millimeter wave small cell network
extractions.metric: data rate, system bandwidth, latency, reliability
extractions.result: VR broadcast feasible with slightly higher bandwidth under ideal conditions

extractions.problem: Maintaining adequate gate coupling ratio and compensating FG-to-FG coupling interference for scaling virtual-ground multilevel-cell flash memory.
extractions.method: Novel bowl-shaped floating-gate structure, segmented virtual-ground architecture with bit-line isolation, and two-step programming with channel hot electron injection Vth compensation.
extractions.finding: Achieved GCR of 0.6, near-complete elimination of Vth shift in word-line direction, and successful implementation of a 3F²/bit test chip with good reliability.
extractions.dataset: Virtual-ground MLC FG flash memory test chip
extractions.metric: gate coupling ratio (GCR), Vth shift, cell size (F²/bit)
extractions.result: GCR = 0.6, cell size = 3F²/bit, Vth shift nearly eliminated in WL direction

extractions.problem: Investigating how 3D virtual reality can improve high school students' mental models of biological cells compared to traditional 2D learning materials.
extractions.method: Inquiry-based 3D VR game with pre/post drawing analysis and biology assessments to evaluate changes in students' mental models and factual knowledge.
extractions.finding: Students' post-drawings showed more organelle types, increased density, and additional complexity, indicating more accurate mental models, but factual knowledge scores did not improve.
extractions.dataset: High school biology students (sample size not specified)
extractions.metric: organelle types in drawings, organelle density, factual knowledge test scores
extractions.result: improved mental models (more organelle types/density) but no factual knowledge improvement

extractions.problem: Enabling virtual commissioning of linked manufacturing cells from different manufacturers with material flow in a 3D integration platform.
extractions.method: Three approaches using NVIDIA Omniverse: (1) source/sink with OPC UA, (2) bidirectional connector with Apache Kafka, (3) bidirectional connector using platform extension only.
extractions.finding: Virtual commissioning of multiple linked digital twins from different manufacturers with material flow is feasible in a 3D platform, contributing significantly to the industrial metaverse.
extractions.dataset: Linked manufacturing cells with digital twins from different manufacturers

extractions.problem: Analyzing performance of dual virtual cell systems under overlay convergent networks with cognitive networking.
extractions.method: STTC-based dual virtual cell (DVC) system with radio resource monitoring; simulation analysis of imperfect CSI effects on performance.
extractions.finding: DVC employment strategy based on resource monitoring improves multi-user high-rate data transmission; quantitative analysis reveals the impact of imperfect CSI on system performance.
extractions.dataset: Simulated overlay convergent network with cognitive networking
extractions.metric: spectral efficiency, effect of imperfect CSI

extractions.problem: Concept, design and operation of periodic virtual cell manufacturing (P-VCM).
Transferring cell type labels between single-cell RNA-seq datasets with batch effects across platforms and species.
extractions.method: scAdapt: virtual adversarial domain adaptation network that trains an enhanced classifier using labeled source and unlabeled target data, aligning source and pseudo-labeled target centroids for joint embedding.
extractions.finding: scAdapt outperforms existing methods for cross-platform, cross-species, spatial transcriptomic, and COVID-19 immune dataset classification, with superior cell mixing and preserved cluster structure.
extractions.dataset: Simulated, cross-platform, cross-species, spatial transcriptomic, and COVID-19 immune scRNA-seq datasets
extractions.metric: classification accuracy, cell mixing, cluster structure preservation
extractions.baseline: existing supervised cell type classification methods
extractions.result: outperforms existing methods across all tested scenarios

extractions.problem: Minimizing energy consumption of user equipment in virtualized small cell networks with mobile edge computing under multiple mobile virtual network operators.
extractions.method: Joint offloading, time slice, and power allocation formulated as mixed integer nonlinear programming; solved via biconvex decomposition with ADMM-based distributed algorithm.
extractions.finding: The ADMM-based distributed algorithm achieves performance close to optimal with higher network performance and better adaptation than tabular multi-agent RL.
extractions.dataset: Simulated virtualized small cell network with MEC
extractions.metric: grid energy consumption, traffic drop rate
extractions.result: performance close to optimal, superior to MRL benchmark

extractions.problem: Developing a virtual aluminum reduction cell simulation.
Simulating virtual cathode oscillator (vircator) behavior including self-consistent anode foil ablation effects on microwave output.
extractions.method: 2D and 3D relativistic electromagnetic particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations with self-consistent anode foil dynamics, plasma expansion, and AK gap closure modeling.
extractions.finding: Microwave output power initially increases then decreases as plasma fills the AK gap (~230 ns, consistent with ~2 cm/μs expansion); dominant output frequency varies from 6.3 to 7.6 GHz; foil damage is higher near axis with rapid erosion phase.
extractions.dataset: Two vircator designs from literature, validated with locally developed PIC codes
extractions.metric: microwave output power, dominant frequency, foil damage profile
extractions.result: frequency shift 6.3-7.6 GHz, gap closure at ~230 ns consistent with 2 cm/μs plasma velocity

extractions.problem: Understanding how heterogeneous myoendothelial gap junction (MEGJ) distribution affects electrical cell-cell communication and flow control in arterioles.
extractions.method: Computational modeling of virtual arterioles varying number, conductance, and distribution of MEGJs to simulate electrical conduction between endothelial and smooth muscle cell layers.
extractions.finding: A minimal number of randomly distributed MEGJs optimizes arteriolar cell-cell communication; heterogeneous MEGJ distributions enable apparently non-decaying conduction in the SMC layer without regeneration.
extractions.dataset: Virtual arteriole model with endothelial and smooth muscle cell layers
extractions.metric: conduction efficiency, membrane potential responses
extractions.result: minimal heterogeneous MEGJs outperform assumed high uniform density for conduction

extractions.problem: Minimizing grid energy consumption and traffic drop rate in virtualized small cells powered by energy harvesters with opportunistic baseband offloading.
extractions.method: Distributed deep reinforcement learning (DDRL) with inter-vSC coordination via battery state information exchange for functional split control.
extractions.finding: DDRL with coordination achieves performance close to optimal, outperforming tabular multi-agent RL in grid energy consumption and traffic drop rate with better environmental adaptation.
extractions.dataset: Simulated network of virtualized small cells with energy harvesters
extractions.metric: grid energy consumption, traffic drop rate, cost savings
extractions.result: performance close to optimal, superior to MRL benchmark

extractions.problem: Modeling tumour growth prior to chemo-radiotherapy starting from a single stem cell to support treatment enhancement studies.
extractions.method: Monte Carlo simulation using biological parameters including cell type ratios, cell cycle phases, Gaussian-distributed cycle times (mean 33h), and natural cell loss.
extractions.finding: The model accurately reflects exponential cell distribution along cell cycle (70% G1, 15% S, 10% G2, 5% M), maintains biological cell proportions (2% stem, 13% proliferating, 85% nonproliferating), and R=3 ratio for clinically detectable to lethal size is consistent with biological data.
extractions.dataset: Simulated head and neck tumour growth
extractions.metric: cell cycle distribution, cell proportion ratios, growth ratio R (detectable to lethal)
extractions.result: R=3, 70/15/10/5% cell cycle distribution, 2/13/85% cell type proportions — matching literature

extractions.problem: Understanding the cellular and molecular basis of innate immune responses in the central nervous system and their outcomes.
extractions.method: Review of CNS innate immunity mediated by resident microglia and astrocytes, including cross-talk with infiltrating T cells and innate immune components.
extractions.finding: CNS immune reactions often occur in virtual isolation from peripheral innate/adaptive interplay; outcomes (infection resolution, neurodegeneration, or neural repair) depend on context of microglia-astrocyte-T cell interactions.

extractions.problem: Determining the cytokine requirements for virtual memory CD8+ T cells across different mouse genetic backgrounds.
extractions.method: Comparison of VM cell dependence on IL-4 and IL-15 in C57BL/6 vs BALB/c mice, using CD1d-deficient mice and infection models.
extractions.finding: VM cells in C57BL/6 rely on IL-15 while BALB/c VM cells rely on IL-4 from NKT cells; this cytokine-mediated control of precursor populations prior to infection influences subsequent virus-specific CD8+ T cell memory.
extractions.dataset: C57BL/6 and BALB/c mice, CD1d-deficient mice
extractions.metric: VM cell frequency, effector and memory T cell responses
extractions.result: BALB/c VM cells rely on IL-4/NKT axis; C57BL/6 on IL-15

extractions.problem: Testing whether the contractile agonist angiotensin II (AII) regulates smooth muscle cell growth via hypertrophy or hyperplasia.
extractions.method: Treatment of quiescent and subconfluent cultured rat aortic smooth muscle cells with AII; measurement of protein content, protein synthesis/degradation, DNA content, and cell cycle analysis.
extractions.finding: AII induces hypertrophy (not hyperplasia) in smooth muscle cells, increasing protein synthesis within 6-9 hours; effect is maximal at 1 μM (ED50 5 nM) in postconfluent and as low as 0.1 nM in subconfluent cultures, with 50% increase in 4C DNA cells suggesting G2 arrest or tetraploidy.
extractions.dataset: cultured rat aortic smooth muscle cells
extractions.metric: protein content increase, ED50, DNA content (4C fraction)
extractions.result: 20% protein increase (postconfluent), 38% (subconfluent); ED50 = 5 nM; 50% increase in 4C DNA cells

extractions.problem: Understanding how CD8+ T cells sense and restrict the HIV DNA reservoir in ART-treated patients.
extractions.method: Correlation analysis of virtual memory CD8+ T cells (TVM: CD45RA+, PanKIR+/NKG2A+) with HIV DNA levels, cytokine profiling, cytotoxic assays, and ex vivo HIV reactivation suppression system.
extractions.finding: TVM cells negatively correlate with HIV DNA reservoir, constitutively express cytotoxic granules, suppress HIV reactivation through KIR-mediated recognition via both cytolytic and noncytolytic mechanisms.
extractions.dataset: HIV-1-infected patients with effective ART
extractions.metric: correlation with HIV DNA levels, IFN-α2 and IL-15 levels, HIV reactivation suppression
extractions.result: TVM cells negatively correlate with HIV DNA, positively with IFN-α2 and IL-15

extractions.problem: Analyzing reverse-link and forward-link capacity in CDMA distributed antenna systems with macrodiversity.
extractions.method: Analytical outage probability derivation for MRC-based macrodiversity with virtual cell concept; proposed new transmission scheme with optimal power allocation for forward-link.
extractions.finding: Reverse-link capacity increases significantly with macrodiversity; forward-link capacity cannot increase with simulcasting regardless of power allocation, but the proposed new scheme achieves rapid capacity increase with more distributed antennas.
extractions.dataset: Simulated CDMA distributed antenna system
extractions.metric: outage probability, link capacity
extractions.baseline: simulcasting with various power allocation schemes
extractions.result: significant reverse-link capacity increase; forward-link capacity increase with proposed scheme

extractions.problem: Inducing pacemaker activity in virtual mammalian ventricular cells.
Finding allosteric EGFR inhibitors to overcome T790M/C797S triple mutation-mediated drug resistance in non-small cell lung cancer.
extractions.method: Structure-based virtual screening targeting the allosteric pocket of C797S EGFR tyrosine kinase, followed by 10 ns molecular dynamics simulation and in silico ADMET prediction.
extractions.finding: ZINC20531199 identified as a stable allosteric inhibitor binding away from the ATP-binding site, with pharmacokinetic predictions within human use range.
extractions.dataset: EGFR T790M/C797S tyrosine kinase crystal structure, ZINC database
extractions.metric: docking score, MD stability (10 ns), ADMET predictions
extractions.result: ZINC20531199 stable in allosteric pocket over 10 ns MD; ADMET within range

extractions.problem: Discovering small-molecule ERBB3 inhibitors for NSCLC driven by CD74-NRG1 fusion-mediated ERBB3/PI3K-AKT signaling.
extractions.method: Sequential virtual screening (pharmacophore and docking) of natural product and Chinese medicine databases against ERBB3 crystal structure, followed by in vitro validation in A549 cells.
extractions.finding: Five of six selected compounds significantly inhibited A549 cell growth; compound VS1 showed IC50 of 269.75 μM, comparable to nimustine hydrochloride positive control (264.14 μM).
extractions.dataset: ERBB3 crystal structure, natural products and Chinese medicine databases, A549 lung cancer cells
extractions.metric: IC50 (cell growth inhibition), ADMET predictions
extractions.result: VS1 IC50 = 269.75 μM; 5/6 compounds active

extractions.problem: Constructing a virtual reality simulation environment for robotic manufacturing cells from scratch.
extractions.method: Semantic robot model with inverse kinematics for 6R arms, bounding-box collision detection, interactive trajectory planning; implemented in C++ with open 3D graphics using VRML models from CAD.
extractions.finding: The system supports enhanced desktop and hybrid wall setups for design and training of robotic manufacturing cells.
extractions.dataset: 6R robotic arm tending a turning centre

extractions.problem: Determining whether grid cells exist in the human brain and identifying their distribution across brain regions.
extractions.method: fMRI during virtual reality spatial navigation, looking for speed-modulated six-fold rotational symmetry in running direction predicted from rat single-unit electrophysiology.
extractions.finding: Grid-cell-like representations found in entorhinal/subicular, posterior/medial parietal, lateral temporal, and medial prefrontal areas; strongest in right entorhinal cortex, with directional signal coherence correlating with spatial memory performance.
extractions.dataset: Human fMRI during virtual reality environment navigation
extractions.metric: six-fold rotational symmetry signal, spatial memory performance correlation
extractions.baseline: rat entorhinal cortex grid cell electrophysiology
extractions.result: grid-cell-like signal found; entorhinal coherence correlates with spatial memory

extractions.problem: Developing a comprehensive 3D model of hair cell mechanoelectrical transduction incorporating gating spring theory.
extractions.method: Virtual hair cell combining full 3D bundle mechanical model with gating spring theory; tip link tension and fast adaptation modulator kinetics determine individual channel opening/closing.
extractions.finding: The model reproduces three in vitro features: transient bundle rebound during fast adaptation, minimized dynamic stiffness at steepest I-X curve, and directional polarity of activation.
extractions.dataset: Turtle utricle hair cells (in vitro data for validation)
extractions.metric: bundle tip displacement, dynamic stiffness, I-X curve slope, polarity response
extractions.result: reproduces transient rebound, stiffness minimum at steepest I-X, and polarity

extractions.problem: Developing a call admission control scheme for ATM networks that estimates cell loss quality for bursty traffic sources.
extractions.method: Virtual cell loss probability estimation using peak and mean rate parameters, combined with traffic clustering and subcapacity assignment; virtual bandwidth method for practical implementation.
extractions.finding: The approach effectively estimates real cell loss probability for heterogeneous/homogeneous traffic when burst length exceeds buffer capacity, enabling QoS-aware call admission control.
extractions.dataset: ATM network traffic models (heterogeneous and homogeneous)
extractions.metric: virtual cell loss probability, quality of service (QoS)

extractions.problem: Characterizing the molecular profile and virtual karyotype of clear cell papillary renal cell carcinoma (CCP-RCC) to understand its genetic basis.
extractions.method: Targeted next-generation sequencing and single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) array on FFPE tissue from 22 CCP-RCC cases.
extractions.finding: Found 30 somatic variants across 63.3% of cases (56.7% predicted deleterious), and copy number abnormalities/LOH in 22.7%; CCP-RCCs are genetically heterogeneous, with some resembling clear cell RCC.
extractions.dataset: 22 CCP-RCC cases from University of Alabama at Birmingham
extractions.metric: somatic variant count, deleterious prediction rate, CNA/LOH frequency
extractions.baseline: clear cell RCC and papillary RCC molecular profiles
extractions.result: 30 variants in 63.3% of cases; 22.7% with CNA/LOH; genetically heterogeneous

extractions.problem: Assessing student and teacher perceptions of virtual laboratory simulations (Labster) in undergraduate cell biology education.
extractions.method: Surveys and interviews with 352 respondents evaluating user-friendliness, motivation, autonomy, and suitability as lab replacement or preparation.
extractions.finding: >90% found VLs user-friendly and visually engaging; >80% reported increased motivation and confidence; >90% preferred VLs as pre-lab preparation rather than full replacement; ~60% noted significant technical resource requirements.
extractions.dataset: 352 first-year undergraduate cell biology students and teachers
extractions.metric: perception survey scores (user-friendliness, motivation, autonomy, confidence)
extractions.result: >90% user-friendly, >80% increased motivation, 72% view as potential replacement, >90% prefer as pre-lab

extractions.problem: Evaluating the effect of mechanoelectric transduction parameters on virtual hair cell response.
extractions.method: Parametric simulation varying gating threshold, critical tension, resting tension, Ca2+ concentration, and number of forced cilia in the 3D virtual hair cell model.
extractions.finding: Gating threshold dominates initial sensitivity; critical tension affects time course and F-X relationship but not peak response; higher Ca2+ reduces fast adaptation time constant; resting tension helps overcome viscous drag during oscillatory movement.
extractions.dataset: Virtual hair cell model (turtle utricle)
extractions.metric: initial sensitivity, peak response (I), time course I(t), F-X relationship, fast adaptation time constant
extractions.result: gating threshold dominates sensitivity; Ca2+ reduces adaptation time constant

extractions.problem: Validating robotic cell layout design and conducting implementation planning using virtual reality before building the real cell.
extractions.method: Development of a semi-immersive virtual environment for a robotic cell with 1:1 scale digital objects.
extractions.finding: The VR environment provides users with a sense of presence and enables working with digital objects as in the real world, suitable for layout validation, implementation planning, teaching, and training.
extractions.dataset: Robotic manufacturing cell
